best_case_thesis: A 1:1 symmetric tight fixed exit (TP=0.5%/SL=0.5%) directly attacks the structural pathology that killed E126 in paper: asymmetric TP/SL pairs with TP=0.51 and SL=6.00 produced an 80% win rate that masked a catastrophic left tail (one loss erased 12 wins). By forcing payoff symmetry, the strategy's expectancy becomes a pure function of directional hit-rate above 50%, which is exactly what BWE 'pricechange' triggers like E126/E064/E066 demonstrably possess (lift_vs_median +0.42 to +0.48 over 3000+ triggers). On 0.5% bands, BWE pump/crash signals — which typically deliver 0.8-2% intraday moves within minutes — should resolve to TP fast, before mean-reversion or wick-out can drag price through SL.
expected_alpha_source: Microstructure exhaust after BWE-flagged taker-buy/sell extremes: the first 30-90 seconds post-signal show high directional persistence at small magnitudes (sub-1%), but degrade past that — a tight symmetric band harvests this short-horizon persistence while capping the fat-tail reversals that destroyed E126.
regime_match: High-volatility, high-taker-imbalance regimes where BWE pricechange/OI signals fire frequently (3000+ triggers/year) and short-horizon directional follow-through is structurally present — i.e., normal-to-elevated crypto vol regimes, not deep ranges.
if_paper_works_likely_reason: The kelly_capped and p25_capped_tail v2 metrics will finally reflect realized expectancy because the symmetric payoff structure removes the legacy p25-blind-spot that made E126 look like 0.3946 while compounding to -13.5%.
could_compound_with: Any sibling X10x exit proposing symmetric or near-symmetric fixed bands at different scales (e.g., X102 at 1.0/1.0, X103 at 0.3/0.3) — together they form a tightness-sweep that lets the synthesizer find the optimal symmetric band per channel, finally giving the exit-side pipeline the same depth as the entry side flagged in R2.
